description: Update the opentabs-plugin readme command to auto-generate a Configuration section in README.md when a plugin has a configSchema. Also fix the empty urlPatterns error for config-only plugins like Grafana.

userStories.title: Add Configuration section to README generation
userStories.description: Update generateReadme() in platform/plugin-tools/src/commands/readme.ts to render a Configuration section between Setup and Tools when the plugin has a configSchema. Read configSchema from dist/tools.json and render a table with Setting, Type, Required, and Description columns. Also fix handleReadme() to handle plugins with empty urlPatterns but configSchema (like Grafana) instead of erroring.
acceptanceCriteria: generateReadme() accepts an optional configSchema parameter and renders a '## Configuration' section with a markdown table when configSchema is non-empty
Configuration table has columns: Setting, Type, Required, Description
Each row shows the setting key, its type, Yes/No for required, and the description field
The Configuration section appears between Setup and Tools sections
handleReadme() reads configSchema from dist/tools.json (top-level field) and passes it to generateReadme()
handleReadme() does not exit with error when urlPatterns is empty but configSchema has a url-type field — instead it uses the configSchema label for the setup section or skips the domain-based setup
Plugins without configSchema generate READMEs identical to the current format (no regression)
All existing tests in readme.test.ts pass
New tests cover: README with configSchema, README with empty urlPatterns + configSchema, configSchema table rendering
